pages.raw_text:
DENVER, Aug. Haywood APP.
peared early at his old desk in the
Western Federation Of Miners
headquarters this morning and be.
gan the task of answering tele.
grams and letters of congratula-
ton.


A lecture bureau offers Haywood
$4,000 for 100 lectures during the
winter. Haywood is considering 1t.


Although the demonstration over
Haywoods arrival last night was
suppressed until it was ascertained
whether John Murphy, the fedora.
ton attorney, had survived the
trip from Boise, thousands gathered
at the depot and pressed so closely
about Haywood that only with
great difficulty was he able to
wheel his invalid wife to the car.
rage.


With his wife safe in the car.
riage he stood up and responded to
the cheers that greeted him. II
was some time before the street
could be cleared sufficiently to per.
NIT the carriage leaving the depot,


In an informal address at the
Albany hotel Haywood mentioned
the threat Of the Mine Owners as
ISOLATION that II he ever returned
to Denver 1t would be in a pine
box. His only regret, he said. was
that Meyer and Pettibone were
not with him as free as he was.


Murphy was taken to his home in
an ambulance after as hearty a re-
ception as his friends dared give
him. He is very weak and failing
fast.
